video_title: Top #DDD Seafood Videos with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: The Lobster Shanty
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm about 30 minutes north of Boston in the famous town of Salem, Massachusetts. You know, the witch trials and everything? Well, right now I'm in the historic area known as Artist's Row. And what caught my ear is I hear there's a little joint that's making a wicked lobster martini. This is the lobster shanty.

dishes.description: A martini made with vodka, lobster essence (from lobster stock), and garnished with a lobster claw and lemon twist.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Great lobster flavor. This is almost like the ghost of lobster martini. Yeah. You don't get the lobster. You just get the flavor. You just get the hint of it. I understand why you serve the claw with it. But you know what? I think for people that really like martinis and like them dirty, that's got it. It's absolutely lobster martini. It's fresh, and we've got some lemon in it. It's good. I'd definitely have it again.

restaurants.name: Joey's Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Lahaina in a strip mall, here to check out a joint called Joey. He's Joey something. And what do you think they serve? No, they're not serving cheesesteaks, and not hot dogs, not burgers. No, it's not an all-American diner with that name. Nope, it's Filipino-Asian fusion. I didn't see that one coming. This is Joey's Kitchen.

dishes.dish_name: Garlic Kawaii Shrimp with Green Papaya Salad
dishes.description: Shrimp sauteed with butter and garlic, served with a calamansi fish sauce and a salad of julienned carrots and green papaya.
dishes.guy_response: Super crunchy. I love green papaya. That shrimp is tender and sweet. The sauce is money. With the delicious little papaya salad blender. You got to eat the head. I see you doing it. Sure. This is how we eat it. Well, you made that look easy. A garlic kawaii shrimp. The kawaii shrimp was one of my favorite dishes in Hawaii. The papaya salad was so fresh.

restaurants.name: Stogie Joe's
restaurants.guy_intro: This is Stogie Joe's. If you're coming to look for that Italian vibe, this is the place to be. It's got a world-class menu, but it's got a neighborhood feel.

dishes.description: Linguini served with a slow-cooked tomato sauce (gravy) made with blue crabs, olive oil, butter, leeks, onion, celery, bay leaf, garlic, peppercorn, oregano, crushed red pepper, parsley, thyme, white wine, and crushed plum tomatoes. The crab shells are macerated through a food mill for flavor.
dishes.guy_response: It is crab. On crab, on crab. You're getting all the flavor you want. Wham! Right into the pasta dish. It's flavor city, really delicious.

restaurants.name: DiGiorgio's Cafe Largo
restaurants.guy_intro: So how do you feed all these East Coast folks when they come to Florida? They come down to Key Largo. If they want some real deal, old school Italian-American food, well, you bring them right here to DiGiorgio's Cafe Largo.

dishes.description: House-made cavatelli pasta with fresh Florida lobster, celery, carrots, onions, garlic, black pepper, brandy, reduced lobster stock, heavy cream, roux, asiago, fontina, parmesan, mozzarella, smoked provolone, and truffle breadcrumbs.
dishes.guy_response: That lobster is out of bounds.

restaurants.name: Stony Creek Inn
restaurants.guy_intro: When you're in Maryland and you're thinking traditional food, well, you're probably thinking crab. Especially the way they steam them and dump a pile of them on butcher paper. And everybody dives in. So I'm 20 minutes south of Baltimore, rolling in for what they call a crab feast at a place called the Stony Creek Inn.

dishes.description: Blue crabs steamed with a house seasoning blend (no Old Bay), served with sweet corn.
dishes.guy_response: It's just fantastic. They're very rich. It's buttery. It's sweet. The salt complements it really well. The seasoning on the crab is pretty outstanding. Wow.

restaurants.name: Pelican Seafood Market and Grill
restaurants.guy_intro: A multi-generational spot in Ottawa, Canada, keeping a seafood tradition alive. It's Pelican Seafood Market and Grill.

dishes.description: Fries topped with lobster bisque gravy, cheese curds, and generous chunks of steamed lobster, garnished with chives.
dishes.guy_response: Wow. I'm getting all that brandy flavor. Next level with the cream cheese and the mascarpone, great fries, delicious dish, well done. Right on. Lobster poutine. This ranks probably as one of the best poutines I've ever had. And believe me, I've had a lot of poutines. The sauce is super silky, and it's perfect.

restaurants.name: La Tostararia
restaurants.guy_intro: I am hanging out at La Tostararia with Chef Fernando. This guy's got a story that you will love. Comes from Mexico, goes to Canada, opens a taqueria, comes down to the United States, opens a carnitas joint here in Grand Central Market, then goes to culinary school, elevates himself in the world of food, and opens up La Tostararia.

dishes.description: A seafood burger made with wild Mexican shrimp and Oaxaca cheese, served on a bun with chipotle aioli, lettuce, tomato, onion, and an avocado-mango-radish salsa, topped with shredded potatoes for crunch.
dishes.guy_response: Good news is that's fantastic. The bad news is the crew's hitting the vending machine. When you bite into it, you get the saltiness of the cheese, the texture of the cheese. The shrimp's cooked perfectly. And then all the accoutrement, the shredded lettuce, the chipotle mayo, the tomato, the raw onion, the phenomenal salsa with the mango and the avocado. I pride myself in my burgers. I'm known for my burgers. That right there, in my expectation level of burgers, that is about as high as they go. Thank you. Outstanding.

restaurants.name: The Grilled Cheese and Crab Cake Company
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in South New Jersey in a town called Summers Point. Now, this is where people cruise through to go down to the boardwalk in Ocean City. But more importantly, to visit this joint. This is the Grilled Cheese and Crab Cake Company.

dishes.description: A grilled cheese sandwich with a crab cake in the middle. The crab cake is made with jumbo lump crab meat, mayo, eggs, lemon juice, honey mustard, sriracha, crystal hot sauce, Worcestershire sauce, Old Bay, celery seed, baking powder, parsley, and panko breadcrumbs. The sandwich uses Monterey Jack cheese and buttered bread.
dishes.guy_response: Crab cake is a little bit creamier than most people expect. Yeah. Crispy on the outside, tons of crab. No question about the amount of crab in there. Nice crunch of the toast. Not too much cheese. Pull it off, ma'am. Nicely done, bro. Thank you. Nicely done.

restaurants.name: Pinky's West Side Grill
restaurants.guy_intro: We're on the west side of Charlotte, North Carolina. Now, you remember the Penguin. Everybody remembers the Penguin. Well, it closed, but the guy, Greg, opened up a new joint over here on the west side. And you know what? This is Pinky's West Side Grill.

dishes.description: A burger with three 5-ounce patties, three pieces of American cheese, house southern slaw, mustard, chopped onions, and house-made chili.
dishes.guy_response: Greg, I'm telling you, it's fantastic. You're attacking this thing. It's like a coyote got a hold of it. Triple G, sir? It's a sloppy piece of equipment, but it's delicious and filling.

dishes.description: Shrimp dipped in a sweet, crunchy corn dog batter and deep-fried.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, yeah. They're so good. It tastes like a corn dog. Sweet, crunchy batter. Straight from the fair. Straight from the fair. Straight from the fair. Not salty.

restaurants.name: Hanalei Dolphin
restaurants.owner_chef: Douglas and Beatrice Allen, Chef Nephi Takenaka
restaurants.guy_intro: If you've been coming to the island of Kauai since the 1970s, you've probably heard of this place. Or have a buddy that's worked at this place, or know people that are huge fans of this place, I mean, it's literally an institution on the island. Now, it's changed hands quite a few times. And the current owners have worked here since 95, bought it in 2007. This is the Hanalei dolphin.

dishes.description: Sushi-grade ahi tuna marinated in a teriyaki sauce and grilled medium-rare, served with rice and drawn butter.
dishes.guy_response: When I got into this, it is perfectly seared. It is literally cold in the center. Exactly how I like it. Little bit of rice to it. And the butter has that little kick of salt. Oh, yeah, absolutely. A little bit of the fat. That is the way to have it right there, my friend. You weren't lying on that one. I wouldn't have done it. Glad you liked it. Here's the thing that I'm happiest about. You didn't take that delicious piece of ahi and hammer the marinade all the way through it. Without question, you really get to taste the fish. I can see why this has been on the menu that long. Well done.

restaurants.name: Fresh Catch
restaurants.guy_intro: It's not just about the variations. It's about the freshness. That's why I'm here in Kaneohe, about 11 miles from Honolulu. This is Fresh Catch.

dishes.description: Small cuts of Aji tuna seasoned with Hawaiian sea salt, kukui nut, Hawaiian chili flakes, Hawaiian chili pepper water, sesame oil, green onion, white onion, and limu (seaweed).
dishes.guy_response: I could sit here and eat this by the bucket full. It's tender. It's fresh. All the ingredients go together well. It's so balanced. It's so delicate. It's so flavorful. You just get the real deal in this, man. I've got a lot of poke on this island as well. That's legit. Thank you very much, my man.

restaurants.name: Hanalei Poke
restaurants.guy_intro: Nothing better than cruising around the island of Kauai with my right-hand man, Hunter. Hunter, what are we hungry for? Poke. Exactly. Now, Dad, how do you find the best poke? What do you look for? Well, I mean, there's places all over. But I got to tell you, you talk to the locals, you look around. And when you pull into a parking lot and you see a line like that, you got to check it out. This is Hanalei Poke.

dishes.description: A poke bowl with sushi rice, fresh ahi, fresh ono, green onion, fresh limu (seaweed), red onion, mango, watermelon, local avocados, fresh mint, basil, cilantro, macadamia nuts, inamona (roasted kukui and red pepper), sesame oil sriracha mix, and chili pepper shoyu.
dishes.guy_response: I've tried all kinds of flavors I've never had in a poke bowl. The mint, the watermelon. That watermelon is so good in there. That is so refreshing. Really nice. The kukui with the mint, game changer. You've got creamy, spicy, salty, nutty, crunchy. The macadamia. The macadamia nut. It makes the salad even better. It really does. It does. That's delicious right there.

restaurants.name: Poke Stop
restaurants.guy_intro: New York City's got its classic pizza joints. And Chicago, well, they got the hot dog stands. Cali got the taquerias. So it's only fitting that Hawaii would have poke. You know, kind of like the Hawaiian soul food, the fresh cubed fish mixed with the Hawaiian salt and the seaweed. So here we are at a joint where a young local chef has taken his culinary education and experience and put his own spin on a traditional Hawaiian favorite. Welcome to Poke Stop.

dishes.description: Cubed salmon mixed with sweet onions, green onions, Hawaiian salt, chili flakes, furikake (seaweed, toasted sesame seeds, dried bonito flakes), sugar, and house-made soy syrup.
dishes.guy_response: That's great. Thank you. Mega gangster fresh. Sweet to salt. A little sour. A little bit of spice. Nothing is overpowering anything else. Dude. You know what the kicker is? Mm. Get that little crunch of the sea salt. One of the best ways I've had salmon.

restaurants.name: Seasons and Regions Seafood Grill
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in the southwest area of Portland, Oregon, to check out a place where the chef came from a long background in the corporate world. And finally, one day, he said he's going to break free. That's right. He's going to do things on his own terms. Do things his own way. He's going to cook from his favorite seasons and his favorite regions. Wait a second. That'd be a perfect name for a restaurant. This is Seasons and Regions Seafood Grill.

dishes.dish_name: Northwest Seafood Cioppino with Dungeness Crab Cake
dishes.description: A cioppino broth with salmon, rockfish, and salt spring mussels, topped with a deep-fried Dungeness crab cake.
dishes.guy_response: That's really good. That broth is fantastic. Typically, when you have cioppino, you know, you like to have some bread or something to dip it into. And this crab cake on top is perfectly crunchy, which would be kind of like the crusty bread going into it. Works incredibly well. Thank you, chef, for wrecking it. Because this is what I'm going to expect now is a little salty, crunchy crab right on top. Something I didn't see coming, but I definitely would eat all day for the rest of the week.

restaurants.name: In Boca a Lupo
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Juneau, Alaska, and every other day, thousands of people get off those cruise ships, and they walk right by this funky little joint where the chef, who's worked through the ranks and studied a little bit of Italy, has opened up a joint where he's scratch-making, hand-making just about everything on the menu. It's Alaskan food with an Italian influence. This is in Boca a Lupo.

dishes.description: Handmade pappardelle noodles served with Alaskan king crab and a seafood bisque sauce, finished with lemon creme fraiche and sorrel.
ingredients: pappardelle (low-gluten flour, egg yolks, olive oil, sea salt)
dishes.guy_response: Delicious. The bisque is really nice and light and refreshing. The crab is not tough. The pasta is the heart of the dish. It is super tender. And the key is the creme fraiche. Two of my favorite things, crab bisque and a pasta dish. Outstanding.

restaurants.name: Broadway Diner
restaurants.guy_intro: You wrote in and sent me to Baltimore. To the kind of places we love doing on Triple D. A big, shiny, family-owned diner serving homemade everything from everywhere. Hey, welcome to the Broadway Diner.

dishes.description: A seafood pasta dish with scallops, lobster tails, shrimp, mussels, and calamari, cooked in olive oil, garlic, fresh tomato, basil, salt, pepper, and marinara sauce, served over pasta.
dishes.guy_response: The pasta's al dente. I go to Italian restaurants across the country. I can't find pasta that's al dente. I have nothing to say, man, other than just wow. Delicious. That's pretty much in the word.

restaurants.name: Fisherman's Market
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Eugene, Oregon, home of the Ducks, University of Oregon. Now, what else can you expect when you come to Eugene an hour and a half off the coast of Oregon? Exactly. Great fresh seafood. That's because they got this joint. Fisherman's Market.

dishes.description: Fresh Dungeness crab cooked in a crab pot, served with lemon juice, butter, fries, and slaw.
dishes.guy_response: But that is hot crab. That's outrageous. Without question, the most seafood-flavored crab really, I think, has to be the Dungeness. Some lemon juice. I'll take a little dip in some butter. That slaw, nice crunch, really refreshes the palate. That's the freshest experience you can get. It's juicy. It's succulent. You don't even need the butter. It's so wonderful.

dishes.description: A pie with a great crust, filled with tender and succulent crawfish, served with sour cream on top.
dishes.guy_response: Not soupy. Not too thick. Tender. There is tender and succulent and the texture of it is awesome. I didn't know about the sour cream thing you were doing but it's excellent.

restaurants.name: The Fish Counter
restaurants.guy_intro: What do you get when you combine a marine biologist and a chef? No, this is not a joke, you guys. I'm really serious about this. You get a funky little joint right here on the main street of Vancouver, Canada that's serving up healthy and sustainably harvested seafood. This is the fish counter.

dishes.description: Fish and chips made with Harrison River Wild Keta salmon, battered and fried. Served with fresh-cut Kennebec fries, tartar sauce, and slaw.
dishes.guy_response: that's got a good amount of flavor. Not oily one bit. And you get some great crunch. Batter's not too much. It's not competing with the fish. Carter sauce. That's a nice balance right there. Fries are legit. Give me the rundown on the slaw. Kale, cabbage, rice vinegar, and celery seeds. Nice juxtaposition to the salty fried crunchy. Nicely done, my friend.

restaurants.name: Pirate Republic
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, and I got this buddy, and he says next time you come into town... Hey, man, check this out. Anyhow, he says next time you come into town, you gotta find a great seafood restaurant because I'm a, uh, what are you again? I'm a vegiquarium. It means the only vegetables in seafood. Ladies and gentlemen, the one and only Vanilla Ice. Dude, what are you, stuck in the 90s? The best decade ever, man. This is Pirate Republic.

dishes.description: A Brazilian seafood stew with golden corvina, shrimp, clams, calamari, and mussels, cooked in a sauce made with blended oil, garlic, onion, bay leaf, salt, pepper, house-made pepper sauce, bell peppers, pureed tomatoes, dende oil (palm tree oil), and coconut milk.
dishes.guy_response: Mmm. Isn't that dynamite? Everything is cooked right, not overcooked. I love the kick. That is really good. Tastes a little Creole almost. It's spicy. It's tangy. The kicker to the whole thing is the coconut milk. That's what gives it that luxurious, that creamy, that silkiness. It's fantastic.
